description: Figure 2 presents first-principles density functional theory results showing the composition dependence of cohesive energy ($E_{coh}$) and formation enthalpy ($\Delta H_f$) for the Fe$_{1-x}$Mn$_x$Al alloy system, spanning from FeAl ($x=0$) to MnAl ($x=1$). The red square markers represent $E_{coh}$ plotted against the left y-axis in eV·atom$^{-1}$, while the blue square markers represent $\Delta H_f$ read from the right y-axis in eV·atom$^{-1}$. Both thermodynamic quantities increase monotonically—becoming less negative—as Mn content rises from 0 to 1.0. FeAl exhibits the lowest values, with $E_{coh}$ approximately $-6.95$ eV·atom$^{-1}$ and $\Delta H_f$ approximately $-0.39$ eV·atom$^{-1}$, whereas MnAl shows the highest values near $-6.57$ eV·atom$^{-1}$ and $-0.22$ eV·atom$^{-1}$, respectively. The parallel upward trend indicates that Mn substitution progressively destabilizes the alloys relative to FeAl. Because FeAl possesses the most negative formation enthalpy, the figure supports the conclusion that FeAl is the most thermodynamically stable phase in the investigated Fe–Mn–Al series.
